name: Ph.D. in Urban Systems

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: For a research doctorate the visible spine is logical: Independent Study I-II feed Pre-Doctoral Research (USYS 792) which precedes Dissertation Research (USYS 790), matching a proposal-then-dissertation milestone flow. However, the only parsed courses are research/independent-study placeholders; the substantive coursework core (theory seminars, research methods, urban-systems electives) that an interdisciplinary Urban Systems Ph.D. should require is not documented, so intellectual progression through coursework is invisible.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the required doctoral coursework, core seminars, research-methods sequence, and a designated concentration set, so the page shows more than research-credit placeholders.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: A flat prerequisite graph (deepestPrereqChain 0) is appropriate for a doctoral program gated by advisor permission and milestones rather than course chains, and the gates shown ('Permission of Program Director', proposal defense for USYS 790) are sensible. The one dangling reference (INTD 799, missingCourseCount 1) is a minor blemish, likely an interdisciplinary research course record that is not linked.
dimensions.suggestion: Add the INTD 799 record and state milestone gates (qualifying exam, proposal defense) explicitly rather than leaving them implicit in course descriptions.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or research competencies are stated, which is a real gap for a doctoral page where outcomes normally frame the qualifying exam, dissertation, and expected scholarly contributions.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish doctoral learning outcomes (independent research design, interdisciplinary synthesis, dissemination) and tie them to the qualifying exam and dissertation milestones.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is 51 but summedTermCredits is only 22 (creditReconcileDelta -29), reflecting the large, variable block of dissertation/research credit (USYS 790 requires a minimum of 10) that is not itemized; per graduate norms this is expected rather than a hard defect. Still, the coursework portion of the 51 credits is not broken out, so a student cannot see how much is seminars/methods versus research.
dimensions.suggestion: Itemize the 51 credits into required coursework, elective/concentration credit, and dissertation research so the large reconcile gap is explained on the page.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Only one requirement group was parsed and it is confusingly labeled (group name 'USYS 790 Dissertation Research' containing an item 'USYS 725 Independent Study I or USYS 726 Independent Study II'), and the sampled course descriptions are administrative boilerplate. There is no visible statement of coursework, qualifying exams, or dissertation timeline, so the page reads as a set of research placeholders.
dimensions.suggestion: Restructure the page into clear sections, coursework, research milestones, and dissertation, with a legible plan and non-boilerplate descriptions of the actual seminars.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: Urban Systems is a highly relevant interdisciplinary field, and the college's ecosystem includes strongly current offerings (GIS, sustainable urbanism, the 'Augmented City'), but this program's own parsed content is limited to generic research courses that reveal nothing about methods currency such as spatial data science or urban informatics.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface the concentration areas and their signature courses (e.g., urban informatics, environmental systems, health, transportation) so the doctorate's current research strengths are visible.

descriptionQuality.justification: The parsed course descriptions are administrative boilerplate ('This designation covers courses for Urban Systems students prescribed by a supervising faculty member'), which is inherent to independent-study and dissertation credits but leaves the page without informative descriptions of any substantive doctoral coursework.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are clear in kind ('Permission of Program Director', and proposal defense for dissertation research) and appropriate for a doctorate, though non-specific, and one reference (INTD 799) is dangling, slightly reducing consistency.

missingTopics.rationale: A doctoral core needs explicit quantitative and qualitative methods sequences; only independent-study and dissertation credits are visible, with no methods course documented.

missingTopics.topic: Spatial analysis and urban data science
missingTopics.rationale: GIS, spatial econometrics, and geospatial data science are foundational to contemporary urban-systems research yet do not appear in this program's own parsed courses.

missingTopics.topic: Urban theory and political economy seminar
missingTopics.rationale: An interdisciplinary Urban Systems doctorate should anchor students in urban theory and the political economy of cities; no such seminar is listed.
Resilience and equity are central to current urban scholarship and to the college's sustainability offerings, but are absent from the program's documented coursework.
Mobility and infrastructure systems are a core Urban Systems research strand that is not represented in the parsed course list.

emergingTrends.trend: Urban digital twins and smart cities
emergingTrends.rationale: Sensor-rich digital twins and smart-city platforms are redefining urban research; the doctorate should offer methods and seminars engaging them, echoing the college's 'Augmented City' course.

emergingTrends.trend: Computational social science and big geospatial data
emergingTrends.rationale: Large-scale mobility, imagery, and administrative data now drive urban inquiry, requiring dedicated computational and geospatial training.
Research funding and policy increasingly center climate adaptation and environmental-justice questions, a natural focus for an urban-systems doctorate.

emergingTrends.trend: AI/ML for urban prediction and simulation
emergingTrends.rationale: Machine-learning models for land use, mobility, and risk prediction are a fast-growing methodological frontier the program should position for.
Co-produced, community-engaged scholarship is a rising standard for urban research ethics and impact and should be reflected in the methods core.

topSuggestions: Publish the required doctoral coursework and core seminars so the page shows a real curriculum beyond research-credit placeholders.
Add an explicit research-methods and spatial-analysis/data-science sequence to the doctoral core.
Itemize the 51 credits into coursework, electives, and dissertation research to explain the -29 reconcile gap.
State doctoral learning outcomes and milestone gates (qualifying exam, proposal defense, dissertation) clearly.
Surface concentration areas and their signature courses (urban informatics, climate/equity, transportation) to convey research currency.
